mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which flows into corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quartile payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate below 0.5% compresses the risk-free discount rate and incentivizes equity risk-taking; when this threshold is active, the S&P 500 generates positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity remains abundant.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal a consumer demand collapse that exceeds the predictive power of either series alone, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE does not significantly predict negative returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the joint signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS signal by more than 50% in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansions when capacity utilization tightens and operating leverage amplifies earnings surprises.
failure_condition: During late-cycle months, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional mean return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1500 thousand units indicates robust residential investment and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through construction and consumer spillovers.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances or the mean return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation (CPIAUCSL) annualized above 4% erodes real earnings expectations and forces a repricing of the equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with a federal funds rate below 1% creates a goldilocks environment of full employment without restrictive monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS growth and low FEDFUNDS fails to outperform PAYEMS alone in predicting positive 5-day returns (incremental R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2% annualized predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days specifically during post-shock recoveries when output gaps are wide and earnings revisions are most sensitive to growth surprises.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 2% annualized, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases.
